sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Atlanta-based Fortune 500 company Genuine Parts plans to split in two
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Co., the Atlanta-based parent of NAPA Auto Parts, announced its plan to spin off its automotive and industrial parts divisions into two separate public companies. The company believes this split will create more agile businesses better positioned for growth and new investments, with tailored strategies for their respective customers and market needs. With a market value exceeding $20 billion, the split is expected to be completed by 2027, requiring shareholder approval.

feed.title: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) Reaches New 12-Month Low Following Analyst Downgrade
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) recently hit a new 52-week low after Wells Fargo & Company downgraded its price target and issued an "underweight" rating. The stock traded as low as $189.16 following several analyst downgrades, despite the company reporting better-than-expected quarterly earnings and authorizing a $600 million share buyback. Insiders, however, sold significant portions of their holdings, with two directors reducing their ownership by over 70%.

feed.title: Enphase launches software-based export management
feed.summary: Enphase Energy has launched new Power Control software for its IQ9 and IQ8 microinverter-based commercial solar systems. This software enables installers to configure aggregate export limits, which simplifies permitting and reduces costs associated with utility upgrades. The solution supports non-export and limited-export installations, leveraging software-defined export management to minimize the need for transformer upgrades or third-party hardware.

feed.title: Nicolet Bankshares stock hits all-time high at 158.37 USD
feed.summary: Nicolet Bankshares Inc. (NIC) has reached an all-time high of $158.37, surpassing its 52-week high, driven by strong investor confidence and a 32.28% 1-year total return. The stock is considered undervalued per InvestingPro data and the company has consistently raised its dividend. Recent positive developments include strong Q4 operating earnings, raised price targets from analysts, and a maintained dividend program.

feed.title: DTE nears deal to serve second major Michigan data center, with more in line
feed.summary: DTE Energy, Michigan's largest electric utility, is close to finalizing a deal to power another significant data center, following a "hyperscale" project for OpenAI and Oracle already under construction. This new agreement is part of a larger pipeline of data center demands, totaling 3 to 4 gigawatts, which presents a financial opportunity for utilities but also raises concerns among communities about increased power bills and environmental impact. DTE maintains that new contracts will ensure existing customers are not burdened with costs, and the utility is preparing for infrastructure updates, including potential new power plants and grid-sized batteries, to meet this growing demand.

feed.title: Blackstone to acquire home services company Champions Group (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) has agreed to acquire Champions Group, a home services provider, from Odyssey Investment Partners. Odyssey and Champions' management will retain a significant minority investment in the company. Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

feed.title: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. Sells Procter & Gamble Company (The) (NYSE:PG) Shares
feed.summary: Rep. Gilbert Ray Cisneros, Jr. disclosed selling between $1,001 and $15,000 worth of Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) shares on January 30th, while also purchasing stakes in several other companies like Hermès, Tencent, and Costco around the same time. Procter & Gamble recently reported strong Q4 earnings, beating estimates with $1.88 EPS, and announced a quarterly dividend. Analyst ratings are generally positive for PG, with an average "Moderate Buy" rating.

feed.title: PJM board approves $11.8B transmission expansion plan
feed.summary: PJM Interconnection's board has approved an $11.8 billion transmission expansion plan, with Dominion Energy's Virginia utility securing $4.8 billion of those projects. The plan aims to enhance grid reliability due to accelerated load growth, particularly from data centers, and integrate new generation, addressing increasing transmission costs that contributed significantly to wholesale electricity prices in 2024. Key projects include a $2.3-billion underground transmission line in Virginia and a $1.7-billion line in Pennsylvania.

feed.title: HP Inc (HPQ) Trading Down 3.12% on Feb 17
feed.summary: Shares of HP Inc (HPQ) fell 3.12% to $18.66 on February 17, placing the stock 47.10% below its 52-week high and 2.19% above its 52-week low. Wall Street analysts have an average one-year target price of $24.08, implying a 29.06% upside, and a "Hold" recommendation, while GuruFocus estimates the fair value at $31.35, suggesting a 68.01% upside. This stock alert was generated by automated technology from GuruFocus financial data.

feed.title: DTE Energy’s 5-year spending plan jumps 20% on data center, reliability investments
feed.summary: DTE Energy has increased its five-year investment plan for 2026-2030 by 20% to $36.5 billion, primarily due to rising data center demand and necessary reliability upgrades. The company secured a significant hyperscale data center contract with Oracle for 1.4 GW and is in advanced discussions for an additional 3 GW. This increased spending will also fund energy storage, renewable energy installations, and grid improvements to enhance service reliability.

feed.title: Why Gartner keeps ranking Salesforce's Informatica a data quality Leader
feed.summary: Informatica, a Salesforce company specializing in AI-powered cloud data management, has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Augmented Data Quality Solutions for the 18th consecutive time. This acknowledgment highlights Informatica's "Ability to Execute" and "Completeness of Vision" for its Intelligent Data Management Cloud (IDMC) platform. The IDMC platform, powered by the CLAIRE AI engine, provides comprehensive data quality capabilities crucial for leveraging modern AI and analytics across diverse hybrid and multi-cloud environments.

feed.title: First Solar (FSLR) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
feed.summary: First Solar (FSLR) is expected to report increased earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ending December 2025, with an earnings report anticipated on February 24. Despite the positive consensus, the Zacks Earnings ESP suggests analysts have become slightly more bearish, resulting in a -1.65% ESP. Combined with a Zacks Rank #3, this makes a definitive prediction of an earnings beat difficult for First Solar.

feed.title: Teledyne RDI ADCPs to support Japan's deep-sea EIA of Polymetallic Nodule Mining
feed.summary: Teledyne RD Instruments has been selected by Deep Reach Technology to provide 20 advanced Acoustic Doppler Current Profilers (ADCPs) for a deep-sea environmental impact assessment (EIA) of polymetallic nodule mining in Japan's Exclusive Economic Zone. These ADCPs will capture crucial data on current direction, turbulence, and sediment plume transport to ensure responsible resource development and meet international regulatory requirements for pre-mining data collection. The project highlights Teledyne RDI's long-standing collaboration with companies like CSA Ocean Sciences and Kiva Marine in providing reliable deepwater monitoring solutions.

feed.title: Moody's Corp. (MCO) call put ratio 1 call to 2.8 puts into quarter results
feed.summary: Moody's Corp. (MCO) is showing a call-put ratio of 1 call to 2.8 puts as it approaches its quarter results release on February 18. The implied volatility for its February call options is at 90, and for March options, it's at 42, reflecting a notable increase compared to its 52-week range of 18 to 49. The company's stock shows an overall analyst rating of "SELL" with a current price of $424.05, a slight decrease of 0.56%.

feed.title: Truist Financial Issues Positive Forecast for First American Financial (NYSE:FAF) Stock Price
feed.summary: Truist Financial has increased its price target for First American Financial (NYSE:FAF) from $76 to $82, maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential 21.3% upside. This follows a strong quarter where First American Financial exceeded analyst expectations with EPS of $1.99 and $2.0 billion in revenue. The company holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with institutional investors owning approximately 89% of the stock.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets stock hits all-time high at 283.17 USD
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets (CBOE) stock has reached an all-time high of $283.17, surpassing its 52-week high, driven by strong revenue growth and consistent dividend payments. Despite being deemed slightly overvalued by InvestingPro, the company recently exceeded analyst expectations for Q4 2025 earnings and received a raised price target from UBS. This reflects strong investor confidence in Cboe's market position and strategic initiatives.

feed.title: Mubadala Investment Co PJSC reports share stake in Pfizer, AbbVie, dissolves in Merck, Dell
feed.summary: Mubadala Investment Co PJSC has reported changes in its investment portfolio, including acquiring stakes in Pfizer and AbbVie. Concurrently, the company has divested its holdings in Merck and Dell. This strategic shift indicates a reallocation of investment focus within the pharmaceutical and technology sectors.

feed.title: How Ford is building more efficient EV batteries with help from a tiny charging startup it bought 2 years ago
feed.summary: Ford is focused on developing smaller, more efficient EV batteries to create more affordable electric vehicles, starting with an electric truck in 2027. This involves rethinking the entire manufacturing process, including the assembly line and wiring. The company attributes many of these battery innovations to Auto Motive Power, an EV charging startup it acquired in 2023.

feed.title: U.S. Food and Drug Administration Accepts Bristol Myers Squibb's New Drug Application for Iberdomide in Patients with Relapsed or Refractory Multiple Myeloma
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb announced that the U.S. FDA has accepted its New Drug Application (NDA) for iberdomide in combination with standard treatment for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma. Iberdomide, a novel CELMoD agent, has also received Breakthrough Therapy Designation and Priority Review, with a target action date of August 17, 2026. The filing is based on positive results from the Phase 3 EXCALIBER-RRMM study, which assessed minimal residual disease (MRD) negativity rates.

feed.title: Medtronic announces first surgery with Hugo™ robotic-assisted surgery system in the U.S. performed at Cleveland Clinic
feed.summary: Medtronic announced that the first U.S. commercial surgical case using its FDA-cleared Hugo™ robotic-assisted surgery (RAS) system was successfully performed at Cleveland Clinic by Dr. Jihad Kaouk. The procedure, a robotic-assisted prostatectomy, highlights the potential of the Hugo RAS system to expand access to minimally invasive surgery and address operating room challenges. Cleveland Clinic, along with Duke University Hospital and Atrium Health Wake Forest Baptist High Point Medical Center, are among the first to adopt the system in the U.S.

feed.title: Mixed options sentiment in Oklo Inc with shares down 3.31%
feed.summary: Oklo Inc (OKLO) is experiencing mixed options sentiment as its shares decreased by 3.31%. Options trading is relatively light, with a put/call ratio of 0.61, indicating more calls than puts. Despite the stock drop, the implied volatility is up, and the put-call skew is flattened, suggesting a modestly bullish outlook.

feed.title: New Keysight software targets 3D chiplet bottlenecks in AI data centers
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies has launched 3D Interconnect Designer, a new software tool aimed at resolving design bottlenecks in 3D interconnects for high-chiplet and 3DIC advanced packages used in AI infrastructure and data centers. This solution automates complex workflows, minimizes manual optimization, and accelerates design cycles by accurately predicting performance and validating against emerging standards like UCIe. It is designed to streamline the process of creating chiplet-based products, reducing development costs and speeding time-to-market for advanced semiconductor designs.

feed.title: Humana's CenterWell acquires MaxHealth primary care clinics
feed.summary: Humana's CenterWell has acquired MaxHealth, a network of 54 primary care clinics and other healthcare services in Florida, serving over 120,000 patients. This acquisition expands CenterWell's reach and is part of a growing trend of vertical integration in the healthcare payer market. While payers argue vertical integration improves care coordination, critics are concerned about potential price increases, reduced patient choice, and payers potentially manipulating medical loss ratios.

feed.title: Ford’s 2027 midsize EV pickup to feature 48-volt system, smaller side mirrors
feed.summary: Ford is actively working to reduce manufacturing costs for its upcoming 2027 midsize EV pickup, aiming for a $30,000 price point. Engineers are implementing cost-saving measures such as a 48-volt electrical system and smaller side mirrors. This initiative highlights Ford's strategy to make electric vehicles more affordable and competitive in the market.

feed.title: Bolt Data Announces Bolt Data Connect, developed on the ServiceNow AI Platform to Unify Equipment Data and Operational Workflows Across the Enterprise
feed.summary: Bolt Data has announced Bolt Data Connect, a new solution developed on the ServiceNow AI Platform designed to unify equipment data and operational workflows. This platform enables real-time field asset monitoring and service automation for industrial equipment manufacturers, allowing them to predict maintenance needs and automate service responses. The collaboration aims to transform reactive service into proactive strategies, improving customer satisfaction and field team efficiency.

feed.title: Registration Momentum Builds Across the Oncology Pipeline
feed.summary: The oncology pipeline is experiencing significant momentum due to the FDA's accelerated approval framework, which has shown positive real-world outcomes for solid tumor indications. This has led clinical-stage oncology companies like Oncolytics Biotech Inc., Incyte, Erasca, Inc., Karyopharm Therapeutics Inc., and Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. to pursue registration-directed study designs for high-unmet-need tumor types. The article highlights key developments and clinical trial results from these companies, including Fast Track designations, new drug approvals, positive clinical activity for new compounds, and strong financial performance.

feed.title: Saudi's Public Investment Fund dissolves stake in Take-Two Interactive
feed.summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has divested its stake in Take-Two Interactive, a regulatory filing revealed, despite the upcoming launch of "Grand Theft Auto VI." The PIF was previously Take-Two's second-largest shareholder, holding around 11 million shares valued at nearly $3 billion. This decision comes even as Saudi Arabia, through the PIF, is actively investing in the video game sector, including a $55 billion agreement to acquire Electronic Arts last year.

feed.title: Earnings live: eToro surges after Q4 profit beat, Medtronic stumbles, DTE Energy pops
feed.summary: The fourth quarter earnings season is nearing its end, with 74% of S&P 500 companies having reported, showing a blended earnings growth of 13.2%. This marks the tenth consecutive quarter of annual earnings growth and the fifth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth for the index. Key companies like eToro, Medtronic, and DTE Energy have released their results, impacting their stock performance, while several major companies like Walmart, eBay, and DoorDash are set to report next. Investors are focusing on continued themes such as AI, trade policies, and consumer economy trends, with a notable shift in how the market reacts to companies missing earnings estimates.

feed.title: Brown & Brown acquires American Adventure
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Dealer Services (BBDS) has acquired American Adventure Insurance, expanding its dealership-focused insurance operations. This acquisition brings American Adventure's network of over 1,500 dealerships under the BBDS platform, enhancing service capabilities for dealers nationwide. The deal follows other significant acquisitions by Brown & Brown, including Accession Risk Management Group in 2025.

feed.title: Precision Computer Methods Inc. Expands Service Capabilities Across Sage and Intuit Accounting Systems
feed.summary: Precision Computer Methods Inc. (PCM) is expanding its service capabilities for Sage and Intuit accounting systems, offering installation, training, sales, support, customization, and enhancements. The company emphasizes a long-standing commitment to building durable relationships with customers, vendors, and employees while focusing on engineering quality and practical value in its software solutions. PCM's approach prioritizes thoughtful system design, ensuring technology solutions align with business needs and support long-term organizational goals, including specialized expertise as a Sage fixed asset consultant and Accpac consultant.

feed.title: Toby Rice: Pipeline Permitting Failure is the Expensive Option
feed.summary: Toby Rice, CEO of EQT, argues that the failure to approve new natural gas pipelines is an expensive mistake, as evidenced by price spikes during Winter Storm Fern. He advocates for urgent permitting reform to ensure energy reliability and allow for more natural gas infrastructure. Rice contends that inadequate infrastructure forced some regions to pay significantly higher prices or rely on dirtier fuels.

feed.title: All eyes on Republic Services earnings amid valuation questions
feed.summary: Republic Services is expected to report fourth-quarter earnings with anticipated EPS of $1.63 and revenue of $4.21 billion, marking a 4.1% year-over-year increase. The waste management giant faces scrutiny over its premium valuation, especially concerning the sustainability of margin expansion and the performance of its Environmental Solutions segment. Investors will also be keen on the company's 2026 guidance as it navigates macroeconomic trends and competitive pressures.

feed.title: Citizens buys boutique M&A advisory firm with energy retail expertise
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group, through its JMP Securities arm, has agreed to acquire Matrix Capital Markets Group, a boutique M&A advisory firm specializing in the energy retail sector. This acquisition, expected to close in the first quarter, expands Citizens' advisory capabilities into a niche market. The move follows several other recent acquisitions by Citizens.

feed.title: Arista CEO Jayshree Ullal blunt on memory chip constraints
feed.summary: Arista Networks CEO Jayshree Ullal has stated that the company is facing significant challenges due to memory chip supply constraints, leading to "horrendous" prices for these components. This situation, which has worsened considerably in 2026, will likely result in a "one-time increase" on selected memory-intensive SKUs for customers, as Arista can no longer absorb the escalating costs. Despite the pricing pressures, Arista increased its full-year growth guidance, expecting $11.25 billion in revenues.

feed.title: Prudential Financial Lags Industry, Trades at a Discount: Time to Hold?
feed.summary: Prudential Financial Inc. (PRU) shares have underperformed the industry but trade at a discount compared to peers, making them an affordable entry point for investors. The company shows encouraging growth projections for 2026 and 2027 and analysts predict a potential 10.1% upside. Prudential's strong international presence, demand for retirement products among baby boomers, and strategic acquisitions position it well for long-term growth.

feed.title: Fund Update: New $137.0M $UL stock position opened by NORTHERN TRUST CORP
feed.summary: NORTHERN TRUST CORP has opened a new $137.0 million position in UL stock, as revealed in a recent SEC 13F filing for the 12-31-2025 report period. This news comes amidst significant institutional activity, with 219 investors adding UL shares and 1,048 decreasing their positions in the latest quarter. Additionally, congressional trading data shows Representative Lisa C. McClain made both a purchase and a sale of UL stock in the past six months.

feed.title: Is It Worth Investing in PPL (PPL) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
feed.summary: This article examines the investment potential of PPL (PPL) by comparing Wall Street analyst recommendations with Zacks Rank. While PPL has a favorable average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.66, the article emphasizes that brokerage recommendations often have a positive bias due to vested interests. It suggests using the more reliable Zacks Rank, which is based on earnings estimate revisions and has a strong predictive track record, to validate investment decisions.

feed.title: Fund Update: New $188.0M $Q stock position opened by NORTHERN TRUST CORP
feed.summary: NORTHERN TRUST CORP has opened a new $188.0M position in Q stock, as per a recent SEC 13F filing for the 12-31-2025 report period. This fund update highlights significant institutional investor activity with 809 firms adding Q stock to their portfolios, and details Q insider trading activity and recent analyst ratings with a median price target of $105.0.

feed.title: KLA (KLAC) Is Considered a Good Investment by Brokers: Is That True?
feed.summary: This article examines brokerage recommendations for KLA (KLAC), noting its average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.77, suggesting a "Buy." However, it cautions against relying solely on ABR due to inherent positive bias in sell-side analyst ratings. Instead, it advocates for combining ABR validation with the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool based on earnings estimate revisions, which currently rates KLA as a "Strong Buy."

feed.title: After Plunging 18.2% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why the Trend Might Reverse for Q2 Holdings (QTWO)
feed.summary: Q2 Holdings (QTWO) has experienced a significant 18.2% drop in its stock price over the past four weeks, but indicators suggest a potential reversal. The stock's Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 27.43 indicates it is oversold, while Wall Street analysts have raised earnings estimates for the current year, leading to a 5.1% increase in the consensus EPS estimate. Furthermore, QTWO holds a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), reinforcing the expectation of a near-term turnaround.

feed.title: Delta (DAL) Is Considered a Good Investment by Brokers: Is That True?
feed.summary: This article examines the reliability of brokerage recommendations for Delta Air Lines (DAL), noting that while brokers rate DAL highly, these recommendations often have a positive bias. It introduces the Zacks Rank as a more dependable indicator of stock performance, emphasizing its reliance on earnings estimate revisions rather than potentially biased brokerage opinions. The article concludes by suggesting caution with DAL's "Buy-equivalent" average brokerage recommendation, given its current Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts See Cardinal (CAH) as a Buy: Should You Invest?
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts currently rate Cardinal Health (CAH) as a "Strong Buy" with an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.25. However, the article advises investors against relying solely on these recommendations due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms. Instead, it suggests using the Zacks Rank, which is based on earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator, noting that CAH currently holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) due to optimistic earnings estimate revisions.

feed.title: Loews Corp. (NYSE: L) Making Surprising Moves in Tuesday Session
feed.summary: Loews Corporation (NYSE: L) saw bullish price action on Tuesday, with shares moving up 1.25% to $110.14. The company, which provides commercial property and casualty insurance, traded approximately 48,741 shares compared to its 30-day average of 809.27k shares. Loews's stock has a 52-week range of $78.98 to $114.9 and has seen a 1.26% decrease over the past week.

feed.title: Fairfax commits $1.65 billion to take Kennedy-Wilson private
feed.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings has committed up to $1.65 billion to fund an all-cash deal to take Kennedy-Wilson Holdings, Inc. private. This transaction, which offers a 46% premium, will result in Fairfax holding a majority economic stake in the real estate investment firm. The deal is expected to close in Q2 2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals.

feed.title: VERIZON COMMUNICATIONS INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, and strategic initiatives. The report highlights total operating revenues of $138,191 million, increased operating income, and significant investments in network expansion and acquisitions like Frontier and Starry. Verizon faces challenges from intense competition, economic and strategic risks, and operational vulnerabilities like cyberattacks, while aiming for sustainable growth in mobility and broadband with cautious capital spending.

feed.title: Moderna Stock Rises After EU Marketing Authorization For mNEXSPIKE
feed.summary: Moderna's stock rose after the European Commission granted marketing authorization for its COVID-19 vaccine, mNEXSPIKE, for individuals aged 12 and older. This marks Moderna's third authorized product in the EU and follows positive clinical trial results showing higher vaccine efficacy compared to Spikevax. The company has also received approvals in the US, Canada, and Australia, and an analyst raised Moderna's price target to $35.

feed.title: Coca-Cola HBC Grants Performance Shares to Incoming People and Culture Chief
feed.summary: Coca-Cola HBC has granted 48,777 nil-cost performance shares to its new chief people and culture officer, Toon van der Veer, under a long-term incentive plan. These shares, valued notionally at GBP 47.04 each, will vest in three equal tranches from 2026 to 2028, contingent on the company meeting specific performance conditions. This move aligns the executive's compensation with long-term shareholder value and reinforces Coca-Cola HBC's pay-for-performance strategy.

feed.title: Habit Burger launches core menu update
feed.summary: Habit Burger & Grill is updating its core menu with retooled sandwiches, improved classic items, and a lineup of limited-time offers, aiming to boost same-store sales which were down 1% in 2025. The changes include reimagined Sirloin Steak and Grilled Chicken Sandwiches, new BBQ Chicken Salad and BBQ Bacon Char, and LTOs like the Baja Crispy Fish Sandwich. This move follows other fast-casual burger chains updating their menus amidst consumer pullback in the restaurant industry.

feed.title: U.S. FDA accepts Bristol Myers Squibb's NDA for iberdomide in multiple myeloma
feed.summary: The U.S. FDA has accepted Bristol Myers Squibb's New Drug Application (NDA) for iberdomide, a treatment for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma. This acceptance initiates the regulatory review process for the drug, indicating potential new therapeutic options for patients with this challenging cancer.

feed.title: Kenvue earnings on deck as Kimberly-Clark merger looms
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. is set to announce its fourth-quarter results, with investor attention focused more on its impending $48.7 billion acquisition by Kimberly-Clark than its operational performance. Analysts expect EPS of $0.33 and revenue of $4.11 billion, showing sequential improvements. However, with the merger approved by shareholders and the stock trading near the implied deal value, quarterly results are unlikely to significantly impact the stock price, as the company will not host a conference call due to the pending transaction.
